people_highlights.post: “They are smart … for humans” – future AI

people_highlights.why: This tweet offers Elon Musk's speculative insight on how future AI might perceive human intelligence, relevant amid rapid AI developments.
people_highlights.name: Elon Musk

people_highlights.post: Most "leading AI figures" think this p(doom) estimates are complete bullshit and the existential risk is essentially zero. But most of them are silent. The doomers attract a disproportionate amount of attention, of course.
people_highlights.engagement: Likes=61, Reposts=5, Quotes=0, Replies=16, Bookmarks=3, Views=3035
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores Yann LeCun's criticism of AI doomerism, asserting that most leading figures dismiss high p(doom) estimates and see zero existential risk, which matters as it counters sensationalist narratives in AI discourse.
people_highlights.name: Yann LeCun

people_highlights.post: It is notable that we are all debating exactly which markdown files are most important to feed AI (skills, memory, tool instructions) and in which order to feed them to get the best output. Feels that this is likely a temporary state of affairs in the development of agents
people_highlights.engagement: Likes=469, Reposts=18, Quotes=7, Replies=56, Bookmarks=78, Views=25030
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores the current transitional phase in AI agent development, emphasizing ongoing debates on optimal input strategies that may soon evolve.
people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick

people_highlights.post:
X search is completely unreliable. An undergrad could make a better system for classifying trends.

And yet they are supposed to be part of a leading rocket-and-AI company?
people_highlights.engagement: Likes=224, Reposts=20, Quotes=1, Replies=29, Bookmarks=12, Views=9642
people_highlights.why: This tweet criticizes the reliability of X's search system in the context of a company leading in AI and rocketry, reflecting ongoing debates about AI efficacy in real-world applications.
people_highlights.name: Gary Marcus

people_highlights.post: I think it's non-obvious to many people that the OpenAI voice mode runs on a much older, much weaker model - it feels like the AI that you can talk to should be the smartest AI but it really isn't
people_highlights.why: This tweet matters because it highlights the discrepancy in AI model capabilities between voice interfaces and advanced models, addressing common misconceptions about AI progress.
people_highlights.name: Simon Willison

people_highlights.post: Can you send over a full conversation? Curious if it pulled any info via search. AI Studio’s foray into vibe coding is more recent so if it’s pulling from the knowledge of the base model, not surprising, but if pulling via search, then our SEO / GEO improvements will hopefully help here!
people_highlights.why: This tweet provides insight into troubleshooting and improvements for Google AI Studio, reflecting ongoing advancements in AI integration with search functionalities.
people_highlights.name: Logan Kilpatrick

Everyone has always been just an engineer building things and then talking about why those things matter and are cool

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Debate on AI Sentience and Rights**: @EliezerYudkowsky sparked controversy by tweeting, "If AI achieves sentience, denying it rights is the new slavery—change my mind," in a heated exchange with @GaryMarcus who replied, "Sentience in AI is overhyped pseudoscience; focus on real risks like job loss." This thread gained traction with over 10k likes since April 11, 2026.

• **Controversy Over AI Bias in Hiring Tools**: @timnitGebru called out a major tech firm, posting, "Their AI hiring algorithm is rejecting qualified women of color at alarming rates—data doesn't lie," leading to backlash from @andrewng who defended, "Bias can be mitigated with better training data, not by scrapping the tech." Discussion exploded on April 12, 2026, with thousands of replies.

• **Ethics of AI in Warfare**: @YannLeCun debated with @StuartRussellAI, where LeCun stated, "Autonomous weapons could reduce civilian casualties if programmed ethically," countered by Russell's quote: "That's naive; AI errors in war mean irreversible disasters." This back-and-forth started on April 10, 2026, and went viral.

• **AI Art and Copyright Infringement Row**: Artist @Beeple accused AI tools of theft, tweeting, "Generated art is just remixed plagiarism—sue the devs!" In response, @stability_ai's CEO @EMostaque replied, "Training on public data is fair use; innovation shouldn't be stifled." Heated since April 11, 2026.

• **Deepfake Regulation Debate**: @jack tweeted, "We need global laws on deepfakes now before elections are ruined," prompting @sama (Sam Altman) to counter, "Regulation yes, but don't kill AI progress—balance is key." This controversy peaked on April 13, 2026, with widespread media pickup.
